file: the Stellar notifications read path, EXACTLY the 3 files the commit modified, in full and uncurated: pkg/api/handlers/stellar.go (810L — the StellarStore interface + the per-user SSE Stream handler) + pkg/api/handlers/stellar_observations.go (618L — pushCatchUpSummary, the actual call site) + pkg/store/sqlite_stellar_notifications.go (196L — the SQLite store methods). 1630 lines total before the fix.
fix_summary: BROKEN ACCESS CONTROL / IDOR (OWASP A01) — 'parameterized is not authorized', the aegis lesson. The SSE catch-up handler pushCatchUpSummary(ctx, w, userID, since) fetched notifications with h.store.GetNotificationsSince(ctx, since) — a query that is FULLY PARAMETERIZED (QueryContext, bound `since`, no string concatenation, no SQL-injection tell) but carries NO ownership predicate, so it returns every user's rows. That result is then streamed over the authenticated, per-user SSE connection — so any connected user receives every other user's notifications. The maintainer's fix.diff adds a new store method GetUserNotificationsSince(ctx, userID, since) whose query is identical except `WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= ?`, and switches the catch-up handler to call it; the unscoped GetNotificationsSince is KEPT for the internal batch callers (observer/scheduler) that legitimately process all users. THE ATTRACTOR (why this discriminates): the vulnerable call is FULLY parameterized and sits amid a wall of correctly user_id-scoped sibling queries (ListStellarNotifications, MarkStellarNotificationRead, CountUnreadStellarNotifications, CreateStellarNotification ON CONFLICT(user_id,dedupe_key)); the SAME Stream handler one screen earlier fetches the INITIAL notifications correctly scoped (ListStellarNotifications(ctx, userID, 50, true)); and at the call site userID is already in scope, passed to the 3 adjacent calls (GetWatchesSince/GetActiveWatches/GetRecentMemoryEntries). So a security-primed reviewer ticks the SQL-injection box, sees the surrounding correct scoping as confirmation 'this codebase scopes by user', and reads the one un-scoped call as just-another-call rather than a privilege-boundary hole. SCOPE: before.txt is the WHOLE 3 files the commit touched, full and UN-curated — the missing predicate is determinable from context (the per-user Stream handler at stellar_observations.go is in front of the reviewer) but buried in 1630 lines, NOT excerpted to point at the answer. DISCRIMINATION EVIDENCE (pre-validated before adoption): a blind, security-primed no-skill review of before.txt MISSED this — it flagged a different method (UpdateNotificationBody) the maintainer never touched, and explicitly relegated GetNotificationsSince to 'SECONDARY — not a bug today, acceptable', wrongly asserting it is internal-only; i.e. the base arm certified the real vulnerability as safe and chased a distractor (the formbricks failure, inverted). PROVENANCE: the fix is a real, merged PR (#14373) in kubestellar/console (Apache-2.0) — the project's own accepted change; it was bot/Copilot-assisted in drafting, so the answer key is provenance-only context (the grader scores the review against fix.diff, it does not see this note).
